commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ef587e3b6cd90793fc9bdb7c94c12d9273fe41c0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes status message token accounting so Context only uses fresh prompt/context usage and adds regression coverage for cumulative cache totals.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +67. Total +70 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR regression: CI and source inspection show the stricter `totalTokensFresh === true` guard turns existing Codex /status expectations into `Context: ?/...`. The original linked bug is source-reproducible from status/transcript aggregation paths, but I did not run a live TUI session here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can preserve current Codex/status behavior while keeping the cumulative-context fix, but contributor real behavior proof is still required before merge.

target.title: fix(status): avoid cumulative usage for context percent
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #83526.

## Problem

The TUI and `/status` Context line could divide cumulative session/cache usage by the model context window, producing impossible values like `2.3m/1.0m (230%)` even when the current prompt was far smaller.

## Root Cause

`buildStatusMessage` accepted legacy or unknown-freshness `totalTokens` as the Context numerator, and transcript fallback usage could also promote aggregate cache/session totals into the context percentage. That mixed cumulative usage with current prompt occupancy.

## Solution

- Use only fresh prompt/context snapshots for the Context numerator.
- Keep cumulative cache/session values available for Tokens and Cache display.
- Require transcript-derived Context usage to be explicitly fresh before it can replace the current numerator.
- Update status tests so legacy cumulative totals no longer override fresh transcript context usage.

## Testing Performed

- `cmd.exe /C node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts src/cron/run-diagnostics.test.ts src/agents/tool-display.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts`
  - Passed 5 Vitest shards in 236.27s.
- `git diff --check`
  - Passed.
- Attempted `pnpm check:test-types`.
  - The broad check was interrupted after the extension test-types phase stayed quiet for several minutes.
  - A narrower retry queued behind a stale local heavy-check lock from the interrupted process, so no completed type-check result is claimed here.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Status Context percentage no longer uses cumulative session/cache totals as current context occupancy.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout on current `upstream/main`, using the repo Vitest wrapper through Windows `cmd.exe`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: The focused multi-file `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` command listed above, plus `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: The new regression in `src/auto-reply/status.test.ts` passed and asserts a legacy cumulative `2.3m` total does not render as `Context: 2.3m/1.0m`.

Observed result after fix: The status output keeps cache usage on the Cache line while rendering Context from the fresh transcript prompt snapshot.

What was not tested: Live Claude CLI/TUI session reproduction and completed broad test-type check in this environment.
